layers.title: Sampling Config Wire Format
layers.summary: The change starts by replacing the single worker-to-client log rate integer with a structured sampling config. Register responses now carry request, access, and diagnostic sampling rates in ppm form, and heartbeat responses also gain the same config message for ongoing propagation.

layers.title: Sampler Core And Flags
layers.summary: On top of the new config shape, this layer adds the `LogSampler` singleton and replaces the first-N `LogRateLimiter` path. Runtime gflags become three bounded doubles and dynamic flag updates commit sampler changes transactionally before publishing an atomic snapshot.

layers.title: Logging Decision Paths
layers.summary: The logging macros and RPC metadata decision paths are then routed through `LogSampler`. FATAL still bypasses sampling, ordinary request logs use request sampling, and PLOG/diagnostic logs use the sampler instead of simply checking severity.

layers.title: Public And Internal Facades
layers.summary: With the sampler in place, the public embedded-worker configuration API changes from one integer rate-limit setter to three double sampling-rate setters. The internal access-recorder API is also reworked into typed Object, Stream, and RequestOut facades so call sites can lazily fill fields only when sampled in.

layers.title: Deployment And Documentation
layers.summary: The rollout layer removes `log_rate_limit` and the standalone client environment-variable documentation, then wires request/access/diagnostic sample rates through config files, Helm templates, dscli docs, and the repository context. This keeps operator-facing configuration aligned with the new worker-authoritative sampler model.
layers.diff:
diff --git a/cli/deploy/conf/worker_config.json b/cli/deploy/conf/worker_config.json
@@ -463,14 +463,22 @@
-    "log_rate_limit": {
-        "value": "0",
-        "description": "Maximum sampled request traces per second (0 = unlimited)."
-    },
+    "request_sample_rate": {
+        "value": "",
+        "description": "Request log sample rate (0.0-1.0). Empty means not configured, use worker built-in default 1.0."
+    },
+    "access_sample_rate": {
+        "value": "",
+        "description": "Access log sample rate (0.0-1.0). Empty means not configured, auto-derive from request rate."
+    },
+    "diagnostic_sample_rate": {
+        "value": "",
+        "description": "Diagnostic log sample rate (0.0-1.0). Empty means not configured, auto-derive from request rate."
+    },
@@ docs/source_zh_cn/appendix/client_env_guide.md
-| 25 | `DATASYSTEM_LOG_RATE_LIMIT` | `0` | 每秒采样请求数上限... |

layers.title: Sampler Regression Coverage
layers.summary: The final layer replaces the removed rate-limiter tests with sampler unit, integration, access-facade, RPC metadata, performance, and system-test coverage. The tests exercise derivation rules, disabled/enabled fast paths, config proto population, sampled-out access skipping, and cross-process sampling propagation.

bcFindings.evidence: The public header `include/datasystem/utils/embedded_config.h` removes `EmbeddedConfig &LogRateLimit(int limit);` and replaces it with `RequestSampleRate(double)`, `AccessSampleRate(double)`, and `DiagnosticSampleRate(double)`. Existing embedded-worker clients calling `LogRateLimit` no longer compile.

`src/datasystem/protos/share_memory.proto` changes field 24 of `RegisterClientRspPb` from `int32 log_rate_limit = 24;` to `LogSampleConfigPb log_sample_config = 24;`, changing the public wire contract for clients and workers built against the previous schema.

bcFindings.symbol: datasystem::AccessRecorder::Record(StatusCode, const std::string &, const RequestParam &, const std::string &, uint64_t)
bcFindings.evidence: `src/datasystem/common/log/access_recorder.h` removes the object-access `Record(...)` overloads that accepted string data sizes and request parameters, replacing direct calls with typed `AccessRecorder::Object/Stream/RequestOut` facades and parameter setters.

`src/datasystem/common/log/access_recorder.h` removes the stream-specific `Record(StatusCode, const StreamRequestParam &, const StreamResponseParam &)` method and replaces it with `StreamAccessRecorder` plus `Record()` after lazy field construction.
`src/datasystem/common/log/spdlog/log_rate_limiter.h` and `.cpp` are deleted and build files remove the `log_rate_limiter` target. Consumers including that class must migrate to `LogSampler`.

summary: This PR replaces first-N log rate limiting with a worker-authoritative random LogSampler, propagates sampling state through RPC/client paths, rewrites access logging for lazy sampled-out skips, and updates deployment docs plus tests.
